title: Alcester & Studley: visit to Studley Campers

main_route.excerpt: Simon, Eve + Tincts went to Alcester to pick up Chad papers from the accountant
then on to Studley to see Studley Campers. Leo has a big tent on his drive. We looked at windows, loos +

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page B_0336_L also contains content from the preceding trip (wedding near Orford, Suffolk, Sept 28th 2019); the October 11th entry begins mid-page
summary: A single-day outing on 11 October 2019 by Simon, Eve and the dog Tinder: from Birmingham to Alcester to collect accountant papers, then on to Studley to visit the camping dealer Studley Campers (run by Leo). No overnight stay; the diary entry occupies a page shared with the tail of a previous trip.
extraction_notes: Page B_0336_L is mixed_start and mixed_end. The first portion of the page covers a Suffolk wedding weekend (28–29 Sept 2019, near Orford) belonging to an adjacent trip. The October 11th day-trip entry begins with 'October 11th Friday Simon, Eve + Tincts went to Alcester...' and trails off mid-sentence; the page text is cut short but no further destinations are implied. 'Tincts' is the family nickname for the dog Tinder. 'Chad papers' likely refers to a charity or trust (Chad being a local abbreviation). Lunch 'in a layby' is noted as part of the day but no specific location is given, so it is not recorded as a stop. Studley Campers appears to be a camping equipment retailer/dealer rather than a campsite.
